page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier memory performance, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 480.23 GB/sec and cached read bandwidth at 7388.06 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25794.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 677.28 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 132.0 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier. Static web serving throughput is also notable at 114.48 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and exceptional memory throughput per unit of compute, making it highly competitive for scale-out workloads. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive database operations, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It delivers 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 480.23 GB/sec for uncached reads, alongside exceptional multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations, where Redis SET throughput reaches 132.0 million operations per second. This profile makes the server highly efficient for scale-out workloads, static web serving, and database management.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 64 Graviton physical cores, 256 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 server with 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for high-throughput database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier database performance, achieving 132.0 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for static web serving, software compilation, and database workloads
